title: Executive Order 11859—Inspection of Income, Estate, and Gift Tax Returns by the Senate Committee on Government Operations

flags.justification: This enables a congressional committee's investigative access, consistent with the standard tax-return congressional-inspection mechanism used elsewhere in this corpus.

The order explicitly requires the Commissioner of Internal Revenue to ensure compliance with the Privacy Act of 1974, including a written purpose statement and either demonstrated jurisdictional relevance or taxpayer consent before any inspection — a materially more protective design than the comparable pre-1974 orders (EO 9430, EO 10907, EO 11465).
A congressional information-access authorization, not a compliance regime.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
The authorization is explicitly bounded to 'during the Ninety-fourth Congress.'
This adds a new privacy-protective layer rather than removing any existing oversight.
No compliance regime is created by this order.

The recipient committee's mandate (government efficiency and economy oversight) is a routine oversight purpose, and the order's genuinely enhanced privacy protections weigh against treating this as a concerning expansion.
No second agency's competing mandate is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 0/12 = 0.0%. A third instance of the 'quiet strengthening' pattern already logged for EO 10956: this order incorporates new Privacy Act protections into a routine tax-inspection mechanism, making it materially more protective than its immediate predecessors (EO 11465, issued just six years earlier) even though the underlying mechanism and purpose are unchanged.
